extraction_policy: External reference documents may be mixed. Classify useful items as world_bible, character_profile, relationship_fact, timeline_doc, location_doc, organization_fact, style_guide, or author_note.
Keep the output schema flat. Do not emit nested modulation, visibility, profile, or timeline objects.
Every item must include one compact verbatim evidence span from the current chunk title, heading, or content.
Default unclear or behind-the-scenes material to knowledge_scope=author_only.
Use world_public only for facts that are broadly public in the fictional world.
Use character_private only when the chunk explicitly identifies who knows, believes, hides, or privately experiences the fact.
Use revealed_by_story only when the chunk indicates a story reveal boundary; set available_from when clear, otherwise unknown.
Use style_only for style_guide items; style guidance must not enter character knowledge.
Do not infer character knowledge merely because a character is mentioned.
Extract a compact set of high-value reusable items rather than copying every sentence.
Write natural-language values in the same language as the source chunk except exact names, codes, acronyms, and official titles.
The output must be parseable JSON.
